ai_strategy.summary: Ohio State University's public AI posture combines published guidance, teaching resources, research centers, AI-relevant programs, and research computing. Public evidence points to distributed governance rather than a verified standalone chief AI officer.

key_provisions: Use appropriate or approved AI tools within institutional data rules
Faculty should set clear course-level AI expectations
Users remain responsible for accuracy, attribution, privacy, and academic integrity
Sensitive data should not be entered into unapproved public tools
ai_policy.summary: Ohio State publishes enterprise AI guidance and teaching resources, while the Translational Data Analytics Institute gives the university a formal AI/data strategy anchor.

bodies.name: AI technology and teaching guidance owners
bodies.scope: Distributed responsibility for AI tool guidance, teaching support, and research coordination.

governance_structure.summary: Ohio State University's AI governance is distributed across IT, teaching support, and research units.

initiatives.title: Ohio State University AI guidance and acceptable-use resources
initiatives.description: Ohio State publishes enterprise AI guidance and teaching resources, while the Translational Data Analytics Institute gives the university a formal AI/data strategy anchor.
initiatives.significance: Institution-level guidance is the baseline signal for responsible AI adoption.

investment.description: Policy and guidance work; no dollar amount disclosed.

initiatives.policy_implications: AI use is permitted within privacy, security, course, and attribution guardrails.

initiatives.title: Ohio State University generative AI teaching resources
initiatives.description: Ohio State University publishes faculty-facing resources for syllabus language, assignments, assessment, and student expectations around generative AI.
initiatives.significance: Teaching guidance moves AI adoption from conduct enforcement into course design.

investment.description: Faculty support resources; no amount disclosed.

initiatives.policy_implications: Faculty set course-level rules and students need clear disclosure and permitted-use expectations.

initiatives.title: Translational Data Analytics Institute
initiatives.description: Ohio State University has AI, machine learning, data science, policy, health, or autonomy research structures that organize faculty and graduate research.
initiatives.significance: Dedicated research structures are a core marker of R1 AI maturity.

investment.description: Research center/institute support; aggregate AI-specific amount not disclosed.

collaborations.description: Cross-school or interdisciplinary AI research ecosystem.

initiatives.title: Data Analytics major with AI and machine-learning pathways
initiatives.description: Ohio State University offers AI-relevant degrees, certificates, bootcamps, or structured learning pathways in AI, machine learning, data science, analytics, or computing.
initiatives.significance: AI credentials connect R1 research capacity to student and employer demand.

initiatives.title: Ohio State University research computing for AI workloads
initiatives.description: Ohio State provides research HPC services for computational and data-intensive scholarship.
initiatives.significance: AI maturity depends on scalable compute as well as policy and curricula.

investment.description: Research computing infrastructure; AI-specific public amount not disclosed.

initiatives.title: Ohio State University responsible and equitable AI access
initiatives.description: Ohio State University's public AI resources stress transparent expectations, responsible use, and access-aware teaching or public-interest AI work.
initiatives.significance: Equity evidence distinguishes mature AI adoption from simple tool availability.

investment.description: Access and responsible-use work; no amount disclosed.

initiatives.policy_implications: Clear AI expectations reduce unequal access, ambiguous enforcement, and privacy risk.

investment.description: Public teaching resources announce the initiative but do not disclose a total budget.

initiatives.title: AI Fluency initiative for undergraduates
initiatives.description: Ohio State launched a university-wide AI Fluency initiative in fall 2025 to embed AI into the undergraduate curriculum, with the goal that every student beginning with the class of 2029 graduates fluent in applying AI in their field.
initiatives.significance: This is a concrete curriculum-scale AI literacy signal with a class-cohort target.

computing_infrastructure.supercomputer_name: Cardinal / Ascend / Pitzer at Ohio Supercomputer Center
computing_infrastructure.summary: Ohio State-affiliated Ohio Supercomputer Center operates AI-capable GPU clusters. Public specifications list Cardinal with 128 NVIDIA H100 GPUs, Ascend with 96 NVIDIA A100 GPUs, and Pitzer with 74 dual-V100 nodes plus 4 quad-V100 nodes (164 V100 GPUs), for 388 disclosed GPUs across these named systems. OSC also publishes AI/ML services such as TensorFlow, vLLM, Ollama, and ComfyUI.

investments_and_gifts.title: Translational Data Analytics Institute
investments_and_gifts.purpose: AI research, education, or infrastructure capacity.

industry_and_research_partnerships.purpose: Interdisciplinary AI research and education coordination.

student_facing_ai.name: Course-level AI expectations
student_facing_ai.scope: Students follow instructor rules and institutional guidance for AI use.

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: Responsible and transparent AI use
equity_and_access_initiatives.description: Public guidance emphasizes clarity, responsible use, and access-aware teaching or research practices.

regulatory_context.summary: Ohio State University operates under federal education, privacy, and research obligations plus campus rules. This sweep found institutional AI guidance but no separate campus-specific AI statute.

narrative_summary: Ohio State now has stronger evidence across platforms, curriculum, and research compute. Approved AI tools include Microsoft Copilot Chat, Google Gemini, and Adobe AI tools; Copilot with data protection opened to students in February 2024 and Copilot Chat is described as covered by the university for faculty, staff, and students. Teaching and learning evidence now includes the fall 2025 AI Fluency initiative, which targets every undergraduate beginning with the class of 2029. Research compute is anchored by Ohio Supercomputer Center systems with 388 disclosed GPUs across Cardinal, Ascend, and Pitzer. Remaining public gaps are active platform usage, contract values, comprehensive AI operating budget, and subgroup outcome metrics.

gaps_acknowledged: Current president, provost, CIO, and chief AI officer were not independently refreshed in this worker sweep.
Ohio State lists approved AI tools and free/covered Copilot Chat access, but license counts, active-user metrics, and aggregate contract values were not disclosed.
OSC GPU capacity is public; AI Fluency outcomes and student subgroup metrics are not yet public.

strategic_archetype.rationale: Primary archetype selected from visible public signals: research compute powerhouse.
